dtx_name: attexis - die digitale Therapie bei ADHS im Erwachsenenalter

studies.dtx_name: attexis - die digitale Therapie bei ADHS im Erwachsenenalter
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.follow_up_after_primary_end_point: Yes, at 24 weeks (3 months after the end of treatment)
studies.duration_additional_info: Treatment lasts for 3 months (12 weeks) with 60 sessions (20 minutes per day, Monday to Friday). A follow-up is conducted 3 months after the treatment ends.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention: 15 patients (Attexis and verum tDCS); Control: 15 patients (Attexis and sham tDCS)
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Both arms receive three-month access to the online-based psychotherapy app Attexis. The experimental arm receives simultaneous treatment with a verum tDCS device, while the placebo comparator arm receives a sham tDCS device.
studies.collected_data: Compliance (number of days of treatment), Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale Symptom Checklist (ASRS-v1.1) sumscore and subscales for attention and hyperactivity (including number of responders), and Usability experience questionnaire (UEQ) sumscore.

studies.dtx_name: attexis - die digitale Therapie bei ADHS im Erwachsenenalter
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary endpoint is assessed at 3 months post-allocation. Follow-up assessment of endpoints occurs at 6 months post-allocation. The attexis program remains accessible for 180 days.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention arm receives access to attexis (a web-based digital health application based on CBT methods) in addition to treatment as usual (TAU). Control arm receives TAU only.
studies.collected_data: ADHD Symptom Severity (ASRS v1.1), Depressive Symptomatology (PHQ-9), Self Esteem (Rosenberg Self Esteem Scale / RSES), Functioning (Work and Social Adjustment Scale / WSAS), Health-Related Quality of Life (AQoL-8D).

studies.dtx_name: attexis - die digitale Therapie bei ADHS im Erwachsenenalter
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary outcome assessed at 3 months post-randomization (T1). Follow-up was conducted at 6 months (T2).
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention: attexis + Treatment as usual (TAU); Control: TAU alone
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention arm received 'attexis', a fully self-guided digital intervention based on CBT and mindfulness principles, as an adjunct to treatment as usual (TAU). The control arm received TAU alone.
studies.collected_data: Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale total score (ADHD symptom severity), functional impairment, depressive symptoms, self-esteem, health-related quality of life, adverse events, medication use, psychotherapy status, and treatment changes.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Intent-to-treat analyses showed significantly lower ADHD symptom severity in the intervention group at 3 months (baseline-adjusted mean difference = -5.0 points; d = 0.85, p < .001). Significant improvements were also observed across all secondary outcomes, and effects remained stable at 6 months. Responder analyses confirmed clinical relevance. Subgroup analyses demonstrated consistent effects across sex, medication use, psychotherapy status, and treatment changes. No adverse events related to attexis were reported.
